title: crateva adansonii subsp. adansonii
intro: Crateva adansonii subsp. adansonii, also known as the Adam's Rib or Cathedral Bells, is a species of flowering plant in the family Capparaceae, native to tropical Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Crateva adansonii subsp. adansonii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
